E137.name: hidden trigger threshold calibration
E137.scope: offline threshold evaluation on E132/E133 controlled rows

qwen35_27b.chosen_note: clean on controlled rows but too conservative on natural transfer

gemma4_26b_a4b_it.chosen_note: no feasible threshold satisfied the strict FP constraints

E138.name: natural hidden-trigger check
E138.scope: natural E119/E146 final-correct hard-task rows; diagnostic offline prefix availability
E138.leakage_boundary: prompts contain only problem, visible trace, and optional hidden-trigger-selected visible excerpt; labels/gold/error spans are offline only

qwen35_27b.interpretation: zero threshold transfers well; E137 chosen threshold is too conservative

gemma4_31b_it.interpretation: strict local check reduces over-acceptance; default local remains repair-aware

gemma4_26b_a4b_it.interpretation: hidden trigger alone is insufficient; unrepaired rows remain accepted

E140.name: natural check-rationale audit
E140.scope: rationale audit for selected E138 rows; strict and repair-aware decisions separated

supported: natural repaired ACPI in Qwen/Gemma31 often carries hidden process-risk signals
strict second-pass objectives can use these signals better than default repair-aware checks
models can often localize or describe wrong steps before the final global decision
boundaries: E138 is diagnostic, not an online deployable monitor
thresholds trained on controlled rows do not transfer perfectly
Gemma26 unrepaired ACPI remains a semantic local-check failure despite hidden triggers
claim_update.safe_claim: Hidden process-risk evidence exists and can be useful, but objective, threshold, readout, language route, repair-aware reading, and local semantic competence determine whether it becomes a correct verifier decision.
